lines.english: Countless the holy, countless the givers.
Countless heroic spiritual warriors, who bear the brunt of the attack in battle (who with their mouths eat steel).
Countless devotees contemplate the Wisdom and Virtues of the Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੋ ਤੁਧੁ ਭਾਵੈ ਸਾਈ ਭਲੀ ਕਾਰ ॥
lines.translit: jo tudh bhaavai saaee bhalee kaar |
lines.english: Whatever pleases You is the only good done,
How can Your Creative Potency be described?
I cannot even once be a sacrifice to You.
Countless silent sages, vibrating the String of His Love.
Countless thieves and embezzlers.
You, Eternal and Formless One. ||17||
Countless fools, blinded by ignorance.
Countless impose their will by force.
Countless sinners who keep on sinning.
Countless cut-throats and ruthless killers.
Countless liars, wandering lost in their lies.
Countless wretches, eating filth as their ration.
Countless slanderers, carrying the weight of their stupid mistakes on their heads.
Nanak describes the state of the lowly.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੋ ਤੁਧੁ ਭਾਵੈ ਸਾਈ ਭਲੀ ਕਾਰ ॥
lines.translit: jo tudh bhaavai saaee bhalee kaar |
lines.english: Whatever pleases You is the only good done,
I cannot even once be a sacrifice to You.
You, Eternal and Formless One. ||18||
Even to call them countless is to carry the weight on your head.
Inaccessible, unapproachable, countless celestial realms.
Countless names, countless places.
From the Word, comes the Naam; from the Word, comes Your Praise.
From the Word, comes destiny, written on one's forehead.
From the Word, comes spiritual wisdom, singing the Songs of Your Glory.
From the Word, come the written and spoken words and hymns.
The created universe is the manifestation of Your Name.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਨਿ ਏਹਿ ਲਿਖੇ ਤਿਸੁ ਸਿਰਿ ਨਾਹਿ ॥
lines.translit: jin ehi likhe tis sir naeh |
lines.english: But the One who wrote these Words of Destiny-no words are written on His Forehead.
As He ordains, so do we receive.
I cannot even once be a sacrifice to You.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੋ ਤੁਧੁ ਭਾਵੈ ਸਾਈ ਭਲੀ ਕਾਰ ॥
lines.translit: jo tudh bhaavai saaee bhalee kaar |
lines.english: Whatever pleases You is the only good done,
How can I describe Your Creative Power?
Without Your Name, there is no place at all.
water can wash away the dirt.
You, Eternal and Formless One. ||19||
When the hands and the feet and the body are dirty,
soap can wash them clean.
But when the intellect is stained and polluted by sin,
When the clothes are soiled and stained by urine,
it can only be cleansed by the Love of the Name.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਰਿ ਕਰਿ ਕਰਣਾ ਲਿਖਿ ਲੈ ਜਾਹੁ ॥
lines.translit: kar kar karanaa likh lai jaahu |
lines.english: actions repeated, over and over again, are engraved on the soul.
You shall harvest what you plant.
Virtue and vice do not come by mere words;
Pilgrimages, austere discipline, compassion and charity

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੇ ਕੋ ਪਾਵੈ ਤਿਲ ਕਾ ਮਾਨੁ ॥
lines.translit: je ko paavai til kaa maan |
lines.english: these, by themselves, bring only an iota of merit.
O Nanak, by the Hukam of God's Command, we come and go in reincarnation. ||20||
Listening and believing with love and humility in your mind,
cleanse yourself with the Name, at the sacred shrine deep within.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਭਿ ਗੁਣ ਤੇਰੇ ਮੈ ਨਾਹੀ ਕੋਇ ॥
lines.translit: sabh gun tere mai naahee koe |
lines.english: All virtues are Yours, Lord, I have none at all.

lines.gurmukhi: ਵਿਣੁ ਗੁਣ ਕੀਤੇ ਭਗਤਿ ਨ ਹੋਇ ॥
lines.translit: vin gun keete bhagat na hoe |
lines.english: Without virtue, there is no devotional worship.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਵਣੁ ਸੁ ਵੇਲਾ ਵਖਤੁ ਕਵਣੁ ਕਵਣ ਥਿਤਿ ਕਵਣੁ ਵਾਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: kavan su velaa vakhat kavan kavan thit kavan vaar |
lines.english: What was that time, and what was that moment? What was that day, and what was that date?
He is Beautiful, True and Eternally Joyful.
I bow to the Lord of the World, to His Word, to Brahma the Creator.

lines.gurmukhi: ਵੇਲ ਨ ਪਾਈਆ ਪੰਡਤੀ ਜਿ ਹੋਵੈ ਲੇਖੁ ਪੁਰਾਣੁ ॥
lines.translit: vel na paaeea panddatee ji hovai lekh puraan |
lines.english: The Pandits, the religious scholars, cannot find that time, even if it is written in the Puraanas.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਵਣਿ ਸਿ ਰੁਤੀ ਮਾਹੁ ਕਵਣੁ ਜਿਤੁ ਹੋਆ ਆਕਾਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: kavan si rutee maahu kavan jit hoaa aakaar |
lines.english: What was that season, and what was that month, when the Universe was created?

lines.gurmukhi: ਥਿਤਿ ਵਾਰੁ ਨਾ ਜੋਗੀ ਜਾਣੈ ਰੁਤਿ ਮਾਹੁ ਨਾ ਕੋਈ ॥
lines.translit: thit vaar naa jogee jaanai rut maahu naa koee |
lines.english: The day and the date are not known to the Yogis, nor is the month or the season.

lines.gurmukhi: ਵਖਤੁ ਨ ਪਾਇਓ ਕਾਦੀਆ ਜਿ ਲਿਖਨਿ ਲੇਖੁ ਕੁਰਾਣੁ ॥
lines.translit: vakhat na paaeo kaadeea ji likhan lekh kuraan |
lines.english: That time is not known to the Qazis, who study the Koran.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਿਵ ਕਰਿ ਆਖਾ ਕਿਵ ਸਾਲਾਹੀ ਕਿਉ ਵਰਨੀ ਕਿਵ ਜਾਣਾ ॥
lines.translit: kiv kar aakhaa kiv saalaahee kiau varanee kiv jaanaa |
lines.english: How can we speak of Him? How can we praise Him? How can we describe Him? How can we know Him?

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਾ ਕਰਤਾ ਸਿਰਠੀ ਕਉ ਸਾਜੇ ਆਪੇ ਜਾਣੈ ਸੋਈ ॥
lines.translit: jaa karataa siratthee kau saaje aape jaanai soee |
lines.english: The Creator who created this creation-only He Himself knows.
